q: You have 250,000 devices which produce a JSON device status event every 10 seconds. You want to capture this event data for outlier time series analysis. What should you do?
opts: Ship the data into BigQuery. Develop a custom application that uses the BigQuery API to query the dataset and displays device outlier data based on your business requirements.
Ship the data into BigQuery. Use the BigQuery console to query the dataset and display device outlier data based on your business requirements.
Ship the data into Cloud Bigtable. Use the Cloud Bigtable cbt tool to display device outlier data based on your business requirements.
Ship the data into Cloud Bigtable. Install and use the HBase shell for Cloud Bigtable to query the table for device outlier data based on your business requirements.
explain:
Correct answer is C as the time series data with its data type, volume, and query pattern best fits BigTable capabilities.

Refer GCP documentation - Bigtable Time Series data and CBT: - https://cloud.google.com/bigtable/docs/schema-design-time-series;

Options A & B are wrong as BigQuery is not suitable for the query pattern in this scenario.

Option D is wrong as you can use the simpler method of 'cbt tool' to support this scenario.
concept: BigQuery, Bigtable

q: Your customer is moving their corporate applications to Google Cloud Platform. The security team wants detailed visibility of all projects in the organization. You provision the Google Cloud Resource Manager and set up yourself as the org admin. What Google Cloud Identity and Access Management (Cloud IAM) roles should you give to the security team?
opts: Org viewer, project owner
Org viewer, project viewer
Org admin, project browser
Project owner, network admin
explain:
Correct answer is B as the security team only needs visibility to the projects, project viewer provides the same with the best practice of least privilege.

Refer GCP documentation - Organization & Project access control: - https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/access-control-org;

Option A is wrong as project owner will provide access however it does not align with the best practice of least privilege.

Option C is wrong as org admin does not align with the best practice of least privilege.

Option D is wrong as the user needs to be provided organization viewer access to see the organization.
concept: GCP Data Engineering

q: Your company hosts its analytical data in a BigQuery dataset for analytics. They need to provide controlled access to certain tables and columns within the tables to a third party. How do you design the access with least privilege?
opts: Grant only DATA VIEWER access to the third party team
Grant fine grained DATA VIEWER access to the tables and columns within the dataset
Create Authorized views for tables in a same project and grant access to the teams
Create Authorized views for tables in a separate project and grant access to the teams
explain:
Correct answer is D as the controlled access can be provided using Authorized views created in a separate project.

Refer GCP documentation - BigQuery Authorized View: - https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/share-access-views;

BigQuery is a petabyte-scale analytics data warehouse that you can use to run SQL queries over vast amounts of data in near realtime.

Giving a view access to a dataset is also known as creating an authorized view in BigQuery. An authorized view allows you to share query results with particular users and groups without giving them access to the underlying tables. You can also use the view's SQL query to restrict the columns (fields) the users are able to query.

When you create the view, it must be created in a dataset separate from the source data queried by the view. Because you can assign access controls only at the dataset level, if the view is created in the same dataset as the source data, your data analysts would have access to both the view and the data.

Options A & B are wrong as access cannot be controlled over table, but only projects and datasets.

Option C is wrong as Authorized views should be created in a separate project. If they are created in the same project, the users would have access to the underlying tables as well.
concept: BigQuery

q: A financial organization wishes to develop a global application to store transactions happening from different part of the world. The storage system must provide low latency transaction support and horizontal scaling. Which GCP service is appropriate for this use case?
opts: Bigtable
Datastore
Cloud Storage
Cloud Spanner
explain:
Correct answer is D as Spanner provides Global scale, low latency and the ability to scale horizontally.

Refer GCP documentation - Storage Options: - https://cloud.google.com/storage-options/;
concept: Spanner

q: Your company is developing an online video hosting platform. Users can upload their videos, which would be available for all the other users to view and share. As a compliance requirement, the videos need to undergo content moderation before it is available for all the users. How would you design your application?
opts: Use Cloud Vision API to identify video with inappropriate content and mark it for manual checks.
Use Cloud Natural Language API to identify video with inappropriate content and mark it for manual checks.
Use Cloud Speech-to-Text API to identify video with inappropriate content and mark it for manual checks.
Use Cloud Video Intelligence API to identify video with inappropriate content and mark it for manual checks.
explain:
Correct answer is D as Cloud Video Intelligence can be used to perform content moderation.

Refer GCP documentation - Cloud Video Intelligence: - https://cloud.google.com/video-intelligence/;

Google Cloud Video Intelligence makes videos searchable, and discoverable, by extracting metadata with an easy to use REST API. You can now search every moment of every video file in your catalog. It quickly annotates videos stored in Google Cloud Storage, and helps you identify key entities (nouns) within your video; and when they occur within the video. Separate signals from noise, by retrieving relevant information within the entire video, shot-by-shot, -or per frame.

Identify when inappropriate content is being shown in a given video. You can instantly conduct content moderation across petabytes of data and more quickly and efficiently filter your content or user-generated content.

Option A is wrong as Vision is for image analysis.

Option B is wrong as Natural Language is for text analysis;

Option C is wrong as Speech-to-Text is for audio to text conversion.
concept: Cloud Storage

opts: Add ", UNNEST(person)" before the WHERE clause.
Change "person" to "person.city".
Change "person" to "city.person".
Add ", UNNEST(city)" before the WHERE clause.
concept: BigQuery

q:
For this Question , refer to the MJTelco case study @ https://cloud.google.com/certification/guides/data-engineer/casestudy-mjtelco;

MJTelco needs you to create a schema in Google Bigtable that will allow for the historical analysis of the last 2 years of records. Each record that comes in is sent every 15 minutes, and contains a unique identifier of the device and a data record. The most common query is for all the data for a given device for a given day. Which schema should you use?
opts: Rowkey: date#device_id Column data: data_point
Rowkey: date Column data: device_id, data_point
Rowkey: device_id Column data: date, data_point
Rowkey: data_point Column data: device_id, date
explain:
Correct answer is A as the requirement is to be able to query on both device Id and date, both of them should be included in the key row. DATE + DEVICE_ID helps define the key row and query for daily data for each device.

Refer GCP documentation - Bigtable Time series schema: - https://cloud.google.com/bigtable/docs/schema-design-time-series#use_tall_and_narrow_tables;
concept: Bigtable

q: You have multiple Data Analysts who work with the dataset hosted in BigQuery within the same project. As a BigQuery Administrator, you are required to grant the data analyst only the privilege to create jobs/queries and an ability to cancel self-submitted jobs. Which role should assign to the user?
opts: User
Jobuser
Owner
Viewer
concept: BigQuery

q: Your company wants to transcribe the conversations between the manufacturing employees at real time. The conversations are recorded using old radio systems in the 8000Hz frequency. They are in English with a short duration of 35-40 secs. You need to design the system inline with Google recommended best practice. How would you design the application?
opts: Use Cloud Speech-to-Text API in synchronous mode
Use Cloud Speech-to-Text API in asynchronous mode
Re-sample the audio using 16000Hz frequency and Use Cloud Speech-to-Text API in synchronous mode
Re-sample the audio using 16000Hz frequency and Use Cloud Speech-to-Text API in asynchronous mode
explain:
Correct answer is A as Speech-to-Text can be used to convert short duration audio in synchronous calls. As well as it is recommended not to re-sample the data, if it is coming at a lower sampling rate from the source.

Refer GCP documentation - Speech-to-Text Sync & Best Practices: - https://cloud.google.com/speech-to-text/docs/sync-recognize & https://cloud.google.com/speech-to-text/docs/best-practices;

Lower sampling rates may reduce accuracy. However, avoid re-sampling. For example, in telephony the native rate is commonly 8000 Hz, which is the rate that should be sent to the service.

Synchronous speech recognition returns the recognized text for short audio (less than ~1 minute) in the response as soon as it is processed. To process a speech recognition request for long audio, useAsynchronous Speech Recognition.
concept: GCP Data Engineering

opts: Grant Team leader group - OWNER and Data Analyst - WRITER
Grant Team leader group - OWNER and Data Analyst - READER
Grant Team leader group - WRITER and Data Analyst - READER
Grant Team leader group - READER and Data Analyst - WRITER
concept: BigQuery

q: Your company wants to develop a system to measure the feedback of their products from the reviews posted by people on various Social media platforms. The reviews are mainly text based. You need to do a quick Proof of Concept (PoC) to implement and demo the same. How would you design your application?
opts: Create and Train a sentiment analysis model using Tensorflow
Use Cloud Speech-to-Text API for sentiment analysis
Use Cloud Natural Language API for sentiment analysis
Use Cloud Vision API for sentiment analysis
explain:
Correct answer is C as Natural Language processing provides pre-model to perform sentiment analysis.

Refer GCP documentation - Cloud Natural Language: - https://cloud.google.com/natural-language/;

You can use Cloud Natural Language to extract information about people, places, events, and much more mentioned in text documents, news articles, or blog posts. You can use it to understand sentiment about your product on social media or parse intent from customer conversations happening in a call center or a messaging app. You can analyze text uploaded in your request or integrate with your document storage on Google Cloud Storage.

Option A is wrong as building and training a senetiment analysis model using Tensorflow would take time and effort.

Option B is wrong as Speech-to-Text API is for audio to text conversion.

Option D is wrong as Cloud Vision is for image analysis.
concept: Cloud Storage

q: A company has lot of data sources from multiple systems used for reporting. Over a period of time, a lot data is missing and you are asked to perform anomaly detection. How would you design the system?
opts: Use Dataprep with Data Studio
Load in Cloud Storage and use Dataflow with Data Studio
Load in Cloud Storage and use Dataprep with Data Studio
Use Dataflow with Data Studio
concept: BigQuery, Cloud Storage, Dataflow

q: Your BigQuery table needs to be accessed by team members who are not proficient in technology. You want to simplify the columns they need to query to avoid confusion. How can you do this while preserving all of the data in your table?
opts: Train your team members on how to query larger tables.

Create a query that uses the reduced number of columns they will access. Save this query as a view in a different dataset. Give your team members access to the new dataset and instruct them to query against the saved view instead of the main table.
Apply column filtering to your table, and restrict the unfiltered view to yourself and those who need access to the full table.
Create a copy of your table in a different dataset, and remove the unneeded columns from the copy. Have your team members run queries against this copy.
explain:
Correct answer is B as the best way to limit and expose number of columns and access is to create a View. With BigQuery, the access can only be controlled on Datasets and Views, but not on tables.

Refer GCP documentation - BigQuery Views: - https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/views-intro;

Option A is wrong as it is not a feasible solution.

Option C is wrong as column filtering cannot be applied to Table and it can be done through Views.

Option D is wrong as it is not an ideal solution, as it results in duplication of data. Also, deletion of Columns is not supported.
concept: BigQuery

q: You are planning to embed a online customer support service within your website. Which GCP Service would allow you to design and integrate a conversational user interface into a mobile app, web application, device, bot, interactive voice response systems, and so on?
opts: Cloud Video Intelligence
Cloud Vision
Cloud Natural language
Dialogflow
concept: GCP Data Engineering

q: You are designing a photo sharing mobile app. Users will upload pictures from their mobile device directly and will be able to share pictures with others. As a compliance requirement, no image with offensive content should be allowed to be uploaded. How would you design your application?
opts: Use Cloud Vision API to identify image with offensive content and mark it for manual checks.
Use Cloud Natural Language API to identify image with offensive content and mark it for manual checks.
Use Cloud Image Intelligence API to identify image with offensive content and mark it for manual checks.
Use Cloud Video Intelligence API to identify image with offensive content and mark it for manual checks.
explain:
Correct answer is A as Cloud Vision can be used for image analysis to detect offensive or inappropriate content.

Refer GCP documentation - Cloud Vision: - https://cloud.google.com/vision/;

Cloud Vision API enables developers to understand the content of an image by encapsulating powerful machine learning models in an easy-to-use REST API. It quickly classifies images into thousands of categories (such as, “sailboat”), detects individual objects and faces within images, and reads printed words contained within images. You can build metadata on your image catalog, moderate offensive content, or enable new marketing scenarios through image sentiment analysis.

Option B is wrong as Cloud Natural Language is for text analysis.

Option C is wrong as the Cloud Image Intelligence does not exist.

Option D is wrong as Video Intelligence is for videos.
concept: GCP Data Engineering

q: You have a large dataset stored in Cloud Storage that needs to be processed using Dataproc. You want to ensure that the storage option is flexible enough to serve multiple worker nodes in different clusters. Which storage option is most suitable for this scenario?
opts: Cloud SQL
Zonal persistent disks
Local SSD
Cloud Storage
explain: Cloud Storage is a Hadoop Compatible File System (HCFS) that is well-suited for serving multiple worker nodes in a Dataproc cluster. It provides flexible and scalable storage for processing large datasets. Cloud SQL is a relational database service, not suited for scalable file storage needed for Dataproc jobs. Zonal persistent disks are tied to specific zones and are not as easily accessible by multiple worker nodes. Local SSDs are attached to individual VMs, making them less suitable for serving multiple worker nodes.
concept: Dataproc, Cloud Storage, HCFS, distributed processing

q: You need to ensure that your data processing system is designed for portability, allowing you to easily move data and applications between different environments, including multi-cloud scenarios. Which technology supports writing data pipeline code that can run on various processing systems?
opts: Cloud Data Fusion
Apache Beam
BigQuery
Cloud Spanner
explain: Apache Beam is an open-source unified programming model that allows you to write data pipeline code that can run on various processing systems, including Dataflow, Apache Flink, and Apache Spark — promoting data portability. Cloud Data Fusion is a managed service with a visual interface but does not offer easy portability across processing systems. BigQuery is a data warehousing service, not intended for portable pipelines. Cloud Spanner is a relational database service, not used for pipeline portability.
concept: Apache Beam, data portability, multi-cloud, pipeline design

q: You want to visualize the transformations applied to data at each step of your data pipeline, providing transparency and accountability. Which Google Cloud tool offers a graphical interface for building and monitoring data pipelines?
opts: Dataflow
Dataproc
Dataplex
Cloud Data Fusion
explain: Cloud Data Fusion allows you to build and monitor data pipelines using a graphical interface, visually representing data transformations as nodes in the pipeline. It also provides a preview mode to inspect data at each stage. Dataflow and Dataproc require building pipeline transformations using code and do not provide a graphical user interface. Dataplex is a data management service focusing on governance and discovery, not visual pipeline monitoring.
concept: Cloud Data Fusion, pipeline visualization, graphical interface

q: You are tasked with designing a data processing system that can adapt to future growth and scalability demands. Which technology can automatically adjust resources based on data volume fluctuations, providing a serverless approach?
opts: Dataproc
Dataflow
Cloud Data Fusion
Bigtable
explain: Dataflow is a fully managed, serverless data processing service that automatically scales resources based on workload demands, making it well-suited for handling fluctuating data volumes. Dataproc is a managed Hadoop and Spark service that requires manual configurations of autoscaling policies and does not scale dynamically in the same way. Cloud Data Fusion enables pipeline creation and management but is not serverless in the same way as Dataflow. Bigtable is a NoSQL database, not a data processing service.
concept: Dataflow, serverless, auto-scaling, data processing

q: A data engineering team needs to ensure that sensitive data in a pipeline is protected. Which of the following is a key measure to protect data at rest?
opts: Using TLS for data transmission
Implementing role-based access control using IAM
Using customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK)
Implementing autoscaling to handle high traffic
explain: Using customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK) allows organizations to control the encryption of their data at rest. TLS is used for securing data in transit, not at rest. IAM is for access control and not specifically for encryption. Autoscaling is a performance and cost-optimization measure, not a security measure.
concept: CMEK, data security, encryption at rest, pipeline security

q: Your organization needs to process a large volume of financial transactions daily, requiring complex transformations before being loaded into a data warehouse. Which data pipeline pattern is most suitable?
opts: Extract and Load (EL)
Extract, Load, and Transform (ELT)
Extract, Transform, and Load (ETL)
Load and Transform (LT)
explain: Extract, Transform, and Load (ETL) is appropriate when complex transformations must happen during transit before loading into the target data warehouse. EL is suitable when raw data needs to be preserved or transformations can be postponed. ELT emphasizes loading raw data first and then performing transformations inside the warehouse — not ideal for complex pre-load transformations. There is no standard 'Load and Transform' pattern.
concept: ETL, ELT, data pipeline patterns, pipeline design

q: You are planning a data pipeline using Dataflow for processing both batch and streaming data. What programming model does Dataflow leverage for this?
opts: Apache Beam
Spark
Hadoop
TensorFlow
explain: Apache Beam provides a unified programming model for both batch and stream processing in Dataflow. Spark is a data processing engine, not a programming model used by Dataflow. Hadoop is a framework for distributed storage and processing but not a programming model for Dataflow. TensorFlow is a machine learning framework, not a programming model for data pipelines.
concept: Dataflow, Apache Beam, unified programming model, batch and streaming

q: Your data pipeline needs to process data from various sources, including structured data from Cloud SQL and unstructured data from Cloud Storage. Which of the following is a common step in a typical data warehouse pipeline?
opts: Storing data directly into Bigtable
Loading raw data into a staging area
Transforming data using only Cloud Data Fusion
Bypassing data cleansing and loading data directly to the warehouse
explain: Loading raw data into a staging area (such as Cloud Storage or directly into BigQuery) is a common step in data warehouse pipelines before transformation and final loading. Bigtable is not typically used in data warehouse pipelines. Cloud Data Fusion is one of many transformation tools, not the only one. Data cleansing is an important step that should not be bypassed in a typical data warehouse pipeline.
concept: Data warehouse pipeline, staging area, ETL, data loading

q: You are developing an ingestion mechanism for a large number of IoT sensors. The service should accept data up to 10 minutes late and perform transformations before writing to a database. Which managed service best handles late-arriving data and performs transformations?
opts: Cloud Dataproc
Cloud Dataflow
Cloud Dataprep
Cloud SQL
explain: Cloud Dataflow is a stream and batch processing service that handles late-arriving data (windowing) and performs transformations. Cloud Dataproc requires cluster management; Cloud Dataprep is interactive; Cloud SQL is a relational database.
concept: Cloud Dataflow late data

q: A large enterprise wants to migrate an IoT platform from on-premises to GCP and use managed services wherever possible. What GCP service would you recommend for ingesting IoT data?
opts: Cloud Storage
Cloud SQL
Cloud Pub/Sub
BigQuery streaming inserts
explain: Cloud Pub/Sub is a scalable, managed messaging queue typically used for ingesting high-volume streaming data. BigQuery streaming inserts are not used for ingestion; Cloud Storage and Cloud SQL are not designed for streaming writes.

q: You are designing a data pipeline to populate a sales data mart. Quality control rules must filter out bad data before it enters the data mart. At what stage of the data pipeline would you implement those rules?

Transformation
Storage
Analysis

q: A team is migrating legacy Python ETL scripts used to transform data. They want a service supporting Python with minimal administration and operations support. Which GCP service would you recommend?

Cloud Dataflow
Cloud Spanner
Cloud Dataprep
explain: Cloud Dataflow supports Python and is a serverless platform. Cloud Dataproc requires cluster management; Cloud Dataprep is for interactive data preparation.

q: You are using Cloud Pub/Sub to buffer records from a stream application. After a developer released new code, messages are now sent repeatedly at 10-minute intervals. What might be the cause?

The new code changed the topic ID

q: IoT sensors send to Cloud Pub/Sub and Dataflow reorders messages by event time. A message is expected from each sensor every minute. If a message is not received, the app should use the average of the last four messages. What kind of window would you use?

Tumbling window
Extrapolation window
Crossover window

q: Your department is keeping some stream processing on-premises using Kafka and migrating some to GCP using Cloud Pub/Sub. How would you share data between the two?

Stream data to a Cloud Storage bucket and read from there
Write a custom service to read from Kafka and write to Cloud Pub/Sub
Use Cloud Pub/Sub Import Service

q: A team wants to create standardized patterns for processing IoT data that multiple teams can use to build streaming data pipelines collaboratively. What component should they use?
opts: Cloud Dataflow Python scripts
Cloud Dataproc PySpark jobs
Cloud Dataflow templates
Cloud Dataproc templates

q: You are working with genetics researchers running a series of six programs where each outputs data used by the next. The final result is loaded into BigQuery from Cloud Storage. What tool would you recommend for orchestrating this workflow?
opts: Cloud Composer
Cloud Dataflow
Apache Flink
Cloud Dataproc

q: Data warehouse owners need to know about all changes to data between points in time, not just the state at certain snapshots. What kind of data warehousing pipeline should be used?

ELT
Extraction and load
Change data capture
explain: Change data capture records each change in a source system. ETL, ELT, and extraction only capture the state of source systems at a point in time.

q: A startup's IoT pipeline streams sensor data into GCP. As soon as data arrives, a Python validation process runs to verify data integrity. Passing data is ingested; invalid data is discarded. What services would you use for validation and ingestion?
opts: Cloud Storage and Cloud Pub/Sub
Cloud Functions and Cloud Pub/Sub
Cloud Functions and BigQuery
Cloud Storage and BigQuery
explain: IoT sensors write to Cloud Pub/Sub. Each message triggers a Cloud Function that runs the Python validation. Cloud Storage is not for streaming ingestion; BigQuery is not for ingest validation.

q: A finance department is migrating a third-party application from an on-premises server. Only the C executable binary is available and it must run on Ubuntu 14.04 with specific packages. Which compute platform would you use?
opts: Compute Engine
Kubernetes Engine
App Engine Standard
Cloud Functions
explain: Compute Engine can run applications in a VM configured with any operating system. The app is not containerized, cannot run in App Engine's language runtimes, and Cloud Functions is for event-driven short-lived code.
concept: Compute Engine legacy application

q: A team is rewriting ETL using a microservices architecture where each microservice runs in its own Docker container. ETL must always finish within one hour. You want to minimize DevOps overhead without sacrificing efficient resource utilization. What GCP compute service would you recommend?
opts: Compute Engine
Kubernetes Engine
App Engine Standard
Cloud Functions
explain: Kubernetes Engine runs containerized microservices with minimal DevOps overhead. Compute Engine requires more DevOps work; App Engine Standard and Cloud Functions do not run containers.
concept: Kubernetes Engine microservices

q: Your consulting company is negotiating a SaaS contract for a client who needs reliable service access. What metric would you use when negotiating to ensure the client's reliability requirements are met?
opts: Average CPU utilization
A combination of CPU and memory utilization
Mean time between failure
Mean time to recovery
explain: Mean time between failure (MTBF) is used for measuring reliability. MTTR measures recovery time after an outage. CPU/memory metrics are about utilization, not reliability.
concept: Reliability metrics MTBF

q: To ensure high availability, your team needs a mission-critical application to run in multiple regions, routing traffic to another region if one becomes unavailable. What would you include in this design?
opts: Cloud Storage bucket
Cloud Pub/Sub topic
Global load balancer
HA VPN
explain: A global load balancer is needed to distribute workload across multiple regions. Cloud Storage and Pub/Sub are not load balancers; HA VPN is for hybrid cloud connectivity.
concept: Global load balancer multi-region HA

q: Sensors on manufacturing machines send metrics including machine type. ML engineers determined each machine type needs its own model for highest accuracy. How should messages be routed from a message broker to ML service instances?
opts: Route randomly to any available instance
Route based on sensor identifier so close identifiers use the same model
Route based on machine type so only one machine type's data goes to each model
Route based on timestamp so temporally close metrics use the same model
explain: Routing by machine type ensures data from each machine type goes to its dedicated model. Random routing and sensor ID routing would mix machine types.
concept: Message routing by machine type

q: An application used by finance is written in Java, deployed on VMs, has several distinct services, and uses SOAP for message exchange. How would you categorize this architecture?
opts: Monolithic
Service-oriented architecture (SOA)
Microservice
Serverless functions
explain: Multiple independent services using SOAP deployed on VMs fits the definition of a service-oriented architecture. Microservices typically use lightweight protocols.
concept: SOA service-oriented architecture

q: A distributed app runs services on VMs where each service implements several functions and it's hard to update one without disrupting others. Your company standardizes on Docker containers. What architecture would you recommend?
opts: Monolithic
Hub-and-spoke
Microservices
Pipeline architecture
explain: Microservices allow each function to be deployed independently in its own container. Monolithic architecture would worsen the update problem.
concept: Microservices Docker

q: The CTO wants to migrate an enterprise data warehouse to a cloud-based data warehouse like BigQuery. What is the first step in this migration?
opts: Assessing the current state of the data warehouse
Designing the future state of the data warehouse
Migrating data, jobs, and access controls to the cloud
Validating the cloud data warehouse
explain: Assessment should be done first. Designing, migrating, and validating all come after the assessment phase.
concept: Data warehouse migration first step

q: When gathering requirements for a data warehouse migration, which of the following would you include in technical requirements?
opts: Data sources, data model, and ETL scripts
Data sources, data model, and business sponsor roles
Data sources only
Data model, data catalog, ETL scripts, and business sponsor roles
explain: Technical requirements include data sources, the data model, and ETL scripts. Business sponsor roles are organizational, not technical requirements.
concept: DW migration technical requirements

q: Your company cannot quickly roll out new BI reports because of the overhead of maintaining an on-premises data warehouse. A lack of BI has been blamed for a drop in sales last quarter. What kind of cost is the company incurring?
opts: Capital
Operating
Opportunity
Fiscal
explain: An opportunity cost is incurred when a missed opportunity has economic consequences. Capital and operating costs require actual expenditure of funds.
concept: Opportunity cost data warehouse

q: Data modelers migrating a data warehouse to BigQuery find their reports require many expensive SQL joins. What BigQuery feature would you suggest to reduce the number of joins required?
opts: Colossus filesystem
Columnar data storage
Nested and repeated fields
Federated storage
explain: Nested and repeated fields can store related data in a single row, eliminating the need for many joins. Colossus and columnar storage don't change join requirements.
concept: BigQuery nested repeated fields

q: Your CTO wants a data warehouse migrated quickly but is risk-averse due to past reporting errors. Which prioritization strategy would you recommend for migrating workloads?
opts: Exploiting current opportunities first
Migrating analytical workloads first
Focusing on user experience first
Prioritizing low-risk use cases first
explain: Prioritizing low-risk use cases allows the team to make progress while minimizing impact if something goes wrong.
concept: DW migration risk prioritization

q: After migrating a data warehouse to BigQuery, the CTO wants to verify correctness before shutting down the on-premises system. What should you include in the verification process?
opts: Verify schemas are correct and data is loaded
Verify schemas, data loads, transformations, and queries
Verify schemas and data loads, and prioritize backlog of feature requests
Verify schemas, data loads, transformations, queries, and feature request backlog
explain: Verifying schemas, data loads, transformations, and queries are all required. The backlog of feature requests is important but not relevant to correctness verification.
concept: DW migration verification

q: You have created a test environment for business analysts to run Cloud Dataflow pipelines. You want to limit the processing resources any pipeline can consume. What execution parameter would you specify?
opts: numWorkers
maxNumWorkers
streaming
maxResources
explain: maxNumWorkers specifies the maximum number of instances that can be run for a Cloud Dataflow pipeline. numWorkers is the initial count; streaming is a mode flag.
concept: Cloud Dataflow maxNumWorkers

q: Your DevOps team is deploying an IoT system using a standard IoT architecture. Which of the following components would NOT be used?
opts: Edge devices
Gateways
Repeater
Cloud platform services
explain: Repeaters are used in networks to boost signal strength but are not part of the standard IoT architecture components. Edge devices, gateways, and cloud platform services are all standard IoT components.
concept: IoT architecture components

q: In the Google Cloud Platform IoT reference model, which GCP service is used for stream processing?
opts: Cloud Storage
BigQuery streaming inserts
Cloud Pub/Sub
Cloud Dataflow
explain: Cloud Dataflow is a stream and batch processing service based on Apache Beam and is used for stream processing in the GCP IoT reference model. Cloud Pub/Sub is for ingestion.
concept: GCP IoT reference model stream processing

q: You are responsible for developing an ingestion mechanism for a large number of IoT sensors. The ingestion service should accept data up to 10 minutes late and perform transformations before writing to a database. Which managed service is best for late-arriving data and transformations?
opts: Cloud Dataproc
Cloud Dataflow
Cloud Dataprep
Cloud SQL
explain: Cloud Dataflow is a stream and batch processing service that supports late-arriving data and transformations. Cloud Dataproc is managed Hadoop/Spark, Cloud Dataprep is for interactive preparation, and Cloud SQL is a relational database.
concept: Cloud Dataflow, stream processing, late-arriving data, IoT ingestion

q: You are designing a data pipeline to populate a sales data mart. The sponsor defined rules for filtering bad data before it gets into the data mart. At what stage of the pipeline would you implement those rules?
opts: Ingestion
Transformation
Storage
Analysis
explain: Transformation is where business logic and data quality filtering are applied. Ingestion brings data in, storage persists transformed data, and analysis is downstream of cleaned data.
concept: Data pipeline stages, transformation, data quality

q: A team is migrating legacy Python ETL scripts and wants a service that supports Python with minimal admin and operations overhead. Which GCP service would you recommend?
opts: Cloud Dataproc
Cloud Dataflow
Cloud Spanner
Cloud Dataprep
explain: Cloud Dataflow supports Python and is serverless, requiring minimal administration. Dataproc requires cluster management. Spanner is a database. Dataprep is an interactive tool.
concept: Cloud Dataflow, serverless, Python ETL

q: You are using Cloud Pub/Sub to buffer user interaction events. After a new code release, messages are repeatedly sent at 10-minute intervals. What might be the cause?
opts: The release changed the subscription ID.
The release changed the topic ID.
The release disabled acknowledgments from the consumer.
The release changed the subscription from pull to push.
explain: If acknowledgments are disabled, messages remain outstanding until the ack deadline expires and are redelivered, causing repeated delivery.
concept: Cloud Pub/Sub, acknowledgments, duplicate delivery

q: Why is idempotent processing logic a best practice when consuming Cloud Pub/Sub messages?
opts: Messages may be delivered multiple times.
Messages may be received out of order.
Messages may be delivered out of order.
Consumers may wait long periods between messages.
explain: Cloud Pub/Sub provides at-least-once delivery, so duplicates can occur. Idempotent logic ensures repeated processing does not corrupt state.
concept: Cloud Pub/Sub, idempotency, at-least-once delivery

q: IoT sensors send one message per minute. If one message is missing, processing should use the average of the last four messages. What window type should you use?
opts: Sliding window
Tumbling window
Extrapolation window
Crossover window
explain: A sliding window preserves the last N events and moves forward as new events arrive, which matches averaging the last four messages.
concept: Cloud Dataflow, sliding windows, stream processing

q: Your team is integrating on-prem Kafka pipelines with GCP pipelines using Cloud Pub/Sub. How should you bridge them?
opts: Use CloudPubSubConnector and Kafka Connect
Stream to Cloud Storage and read from there
Write a custom service to read Kafka and write Pub/Sub
Use Cloud Pub/Sub Import Service
explain: CloudPubSubConnector with Kafka Connect is the maintained integration path. Custom bridges add maintenance overhead, and Cloud Pub/Sub Import Service does not exist.
concept: Kafka Connect, CloudPubSubConnector, hybrid pipelines

q: A team wants reusable standardized patterns for IoT streaming pipelines across multiple teams. What component should they use?
opts: Cloud Dataflow Python scripts
Cloud Dataproc PySpark jobs
Cloud Dataflow templates
Cloud Dataproc templates
explain: Cloud Dataflow templates encapsulate pipeline patterns with parameters for reuse, enabling collaboration without modifying code each time.
concept: Cloud Dataflow templates, reusable patterns

q: You must run several Hadoop map-reduce jobs, one Pig job, and four PySpark jobs in GCP. What configuration is recommended?
opts: Use one cluster for all jobs.
Use three persistent clusters by job type.
Use one cluster per job and keep them running.
Use one cluster per job and shut it down when complete.
explain: Ephemeral per-job clusters minimize cost and simplify operations. Persistent clusters increase idle cost and complexity.
concept: Cloud Dataproc, ephemeral clusters, cost optimization

q: Genetics researchers run six sequential programs on data in Cloud Storage, with final output loaded to BigQuery. What tool should orchestrate this workflow?
opts: Cloud Composer
Cloud Dataflow
Apache Flink
Cloud Dataproc
explain: Cloud Composer (managed Airflow) is designed for orchestrating multi-step workflows with dependencies.
concept: Cloud Composer, workflow orchestration

q: Business owners need data warehouse history of every change between snapshots, not just point-in-time states. What pipeline approach should be used?
opts: ETL
ELT
Extraction and load
Change data capture
explain: Change data capture records each data change event over time, whereas ETL/ELT/extract-load capture state at extraction time.
concept: CDC, change data capture

q: You need to ingest IoT sensor data and run a Python validation check before continuing pipeline processing. What approach would you use?
opts: Store in Cloud Storage first, then process.
IoT sensors write to Cloud Pub/Sub; a Cloud Function runs Python validation.
IoT sensors write to BigQuery, then Cloud Functions validates.
IoT sensors write to Cloud Storage, and Cloud Dataflow validates.
explain: Cloud Pub/Sub supports streaming ingestion and can trigger Cloud Functions for lightweight event-driven validation in Python.
concept: Cloud Functions, Cloud Pub/Sub, ingestion validation

q: You are migrating a C binary app that must run on Ubuntu 14.04 with specific packages. It is not containerized. What GCP compute service is best?
opts: Compute Engine
Kubernetes Engine
App Engine Standard
Cloud Functions
explain: Compute Engine gives full OS control, allowing required Ubuntu version and package installation. The app is not containerized and not suitable for App Engine Standard or Functions.
concept: Compute Engine, legacy migration, custom OS

q: You are building Docker-based microservices for an ETL process and want minimal DevOps overhead. Which service should you use?
opts: Compute Engine
Kubernetes Engine
App Engine Standard
Cloud Functions
explain: Kubernetes Engine is the managed platform for containerized microservices and balances operational simplicity with container orchestration.
concept: GKE, Docker, microservices

q: You need a reliability metric for an SLA to measure service reliability. Which metric should you use?
opts: CPU utilization
Throughput utilization
Mean time between failure
Mean time to recovery
explain: MTBF measures reliability directly. CPU and throughput are utilization metrics; MTTR measures restoration speed, not reliability probability.
concept: SLA, reliability, MTBF

q: A system must remain available across regions and route traffic away from failed regions. What component is required?
opts: Cloud Storage bucket
Cloud Pub/Sub topic
Global load balancer
HA VPN
explain: Global load balancers distribute traffic across regions and can reroute around regional failures.
concept: Global load balancing, multi-region availability

q: Machine sensors send metrics to a message broker. Different machine types require separate ML models due to different failure characteristics. How should messages be routed?
opts: Random routing
Route by sensor ID proximity
Route by machine type
Route by timestamp

concept: Message routing, ML specialization, machine type

q: An application has independent services using SOAP deployed on VMs. What architecture type is this?
opts: Monolithic
Service-oriented architecture (SOA)
Microservice
Serverless
explain: SOAP-based independent services on VMs are characteristic of SOA.
concept: SOA, distributed architecture, SOAP

q: You want independent functions that can be deployed in separate containers and updated independently. Which architecture pattern should you use?
opts: Monolithic
Hub-and-spoke
Microservices
Pipeline architecture
explain: Microservices allow independent deployment and lifecycle of components, often in separate containers.
concept: Microservices, independent deployment

q: What is the first step in migrating an on-premises data warehouse to GCP?
opts: Assessment
Design future state
Migrate data and jobs
Validate migration

q: A data warehouse migration plan should include which items?
opts: Data sources, data model, ETL scripts
Business sponsor names, data sources, data loads
Data sources, ETL scripts, business sponsor names
Only data sources and schemas
explain: Technical migration planning must include source systems, data model, and ETL logic. Sponsor names are not core technical migration artifacts.

q: A reporting backlog exists because the current on-prem warehouse is too limited, preventing valuable new reports. What kind of cost is this?
opts: Capital cost
Operating cost
Opportunity cost
Sunk cost

q: When migrating normalized on-prem schemas with many joins to BigQuery, what feature should you use to optimize design?
opts: Colossus filesystem
Columnar storage
Denormalization with nested and repeated fields
Federated storage
explain: Nested and repeated fields reduce joins by storing related data together, improving performance in BigQuery.

q: For a risk-averse migration strategy, how should workloads be prioritized first?
opts: Highest value opportunities
Analytical workloads first
User experience first
Low-risk use cases first
explain: Starting with low-risk use cases helps teams learn cloud migration patterns while minimizing potential impact.
concept: Migration prioritization, risk management

q: After migrating a data warehouse to BigQuery, which tasks should be performed to verify success?
opts: Verify schemas and loads only
Verify schemas, loads, transformations, and queries
Verify schemas and feature backlog
Verify schemas and user-request backlog
explain: Validation requires end-to-end checks: schema correctness, complete data loading, correct transformations, and expected query/report results.

opts: A TPU pod
A single TPU
A single server with a GPU
A managed instance group of GPU VMs
explain: GPUs are suitable for high-precision arithmetic and interactive iterative development on moderate dataset sizes. TPUs are optimized for low-precision tensor operations.

q: You are training a TensorFlow model with synchronous distributed training on TPUs. Which distribution strategy should you use?

CentralStorageStrategy
MultiWorkerMirroredStrategy
TPUStrategy

q: A model service runs in us-west1, but users in London and New York report prediction response times are too slow. What type of issue is this?
opts: Overfitting
Underfitting
Latency
Scalability
explain: Geographic distance adds network round-trip delay, causing latency issues in inference serving.

q: A global climate project has limited/unreliable bandwidth from gateways to GCP. Sensors send one reading per second, but scientists only need one-minute averages for temperature/pressure/humidity. Which strategy should you implement?
opts: Send all raw data to Pub/Sub and average in Dataflow
Average at gateway, send averages to Pub/Sub, then Dataflow and BigQuery
Send all raw data to BigQuery streaming inserts
Average at gateway, send averages to Pub/Sub, then Dataflow and BigQuery
explain: Compute one-minute aggregates at the gateway to reduce bandwidth, then send compact summaries upstream for further processing and storage.
concept: Edge aggregation, bandwidth optimization, IoT

q: In a standard IoT architecture, which component would you not use?
opts: Edge devices
Gateways
Repeater
Cloud platform services

opts: On GKE with TFJob
On Compute Engine MIGs
On Edge TPU devices in vehicles
On GPUs in vehicles

q: In the Google Cloud IoT reference model, which service is used for stream processing?
opts: Cloud Storage
BigQuery streaming inserts
Cloud Pub/Sub
Cloud Dataflow
explain: Cloud Dataflow performs stream and batch processing. Pub/Sub is for ingestion.

TPU pod
GKE CPU cluster
App Engine

TPU pod
Compute Engine with CPUs
Cloud Functions
explain: A precompiled high-precision Fortran GPU library is best matched with GPU-enabled Compute Engine. TPUs are not general-purpose and focus on low-precision tensor ops.
